channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Everything leaving Netflix in March 2025 – catch Sixteen Candles, Mad Max: Fury Road, and more before they're gone
item.description: Netflix has started its spring cleaning, so here are all the movies and shows leaving Netflix in March 2025.
item.dc:creator: rowan.davies@futurenet.com (Rowan Davies), TechRadar

item.title: TrapC proposal to fix C/C++ memory safety
item.description: TrapC, a fork of the C language, is being developed as a potential solution for memory safety issues that have hindered the C and C++ languages. TrapC is a proposed C-language extension compiler intended to be implemented as a cybersecurity compiler for C and C++ code, said developer Robin Rowe.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: AI priorities: IT leaders emphasize productivity over innovation
item.description: IT leaders are still aiming for the low-hanging fruit when using AI, zeroing in on improving employee productivity rather than more advanced uses such as enabling innovation or expanding revenue, according to a new survey.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: The rising threat of shadow AI
item.description: Employees in a large financial organization began developing AI tools to automate time-consuming tasks such as weekly report generation. They didn’t think about what could go wrong. Within a few months, unauthorized applications skyrocketed from just a couple to 65.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Amazon claims to cut quantum computing timeline by 5 years with new Ocelot chip
item.description: Amazon Web Services has unveiled a new quantum computing chip, Ocelot, that the company claims could reduce error correction costs by up to 90% compared to traditional approaches.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: 7 ways to fight back against spam phone calls
item.description: Unwanted phone calls are out of control. Whether it’s a robocall trying to sell you something or spam calls from scammers trying to rip you off, it’s enough to make you want to stop answering your phone.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Gabbard decries Britain's reported demand for Apple to provide backdoor access to users' cloud data
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Director of National Intelligence Tulsi Gabbard says she has serious concerns about the British government's reported demand that Apple provide backdoor access to any data stored in the cloud.
item.dc:creator: DAVID KLEPPER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Meta apologizes for 'error' that resulted in violent content flooding Instagram feeds
item.description: Meta, the parent company of Facebook and Instagram, apologized Thursday for a technical error that resulted in some users' Reels feeds being filled with graphic or violent content.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: One Tech Tip: Getting a lot of unwanted phone calls? Here are ways to stop them
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Unwanted phone calls are out of control. Whether it's a robocall trying to sell you something or spam calls from scammers trying to rip you off, it's enough to make you want to stop answering your phone.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Microsoft calls on Trump to loosen AI chip export restrictions
item.description: Microsoft is calling on the Trump administration to loosen Biden-era restrictions on artificial intelligence (AI) chip exports that it argues could undermine U.S. leadership on the technology.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Red Hat OpenShift improves virtualization support
item.description: Red Hat OpenShift 4.18, the latest version of the company’s Kubernetes-powered application platform, has arrived, with enhancements designed to simplify management of virtual machines and containers.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Get started with async in Python
item.description: Asynchronous programming, or async, is a feature of many modern languages that allows a program to juggle multiple operations without waiting or getting hung up on any one of them.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: 11 rules for writing better code
item.description: You can’t develop software and manage software projects for 30 years and not learn a thing or two or twelve about writing good code. Here are a near-dozen nuggets of coding wisdom I picked up along the way.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: IBM acquires DataStax to boost watsonx’s generative AI capabilities
item.description: IBM has announced its intent to acquire database and related services provider DataStax for an undisclosed sum in an effort to boost watsonx’s generative AI capabilities.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: IBM targets AI application growth with DataStax buy
item.description: Looking to bolster its AI data handling and storage capabilities, IBM said it had entered an agreement to buy open-source database developer DataStax for an undisclosed amount.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: How VR technology is curing loneliness in seniors
item.description: VR transforms senior care: Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson says immersive tech helps older adults explore and connect via virtual experiences.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Protecting US supply chains from foreign influence
item.description: With the new Trump administration likely to place an even greater focus on reducing reliance on China, this trend is expected to accelerate. The post Protecting US supply chains from foreign influence first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: Top Apple exec concedes he had doubts about app store fees imposed on alternative payment options
item.description: OAKLAND, Calif. (AP) — Longtime Apple executive Phil Schiller on Monday conceded he initially opposed fees that the iPhone maker charges for in app-transactions processed outside its own payment system because he feared violating a court-ordered mandate designed to create more competition.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Elizabeth Holmes fails to overturn her Theranos fraud conviction
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Elizabeth Holmes, the disgraced founder of Theranos, will remain in prison after losing a bid Monday to overturn her fraud conviction, with a federal appeals court saying she hadn’t proved there were legal missteps during her trial for defrauding investors with false claims of w
item.dc:creator: OLGA R. RODRIGUEZ, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Apple announces $500 billion investment in US amid tariff threats that could affect the iPhone
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Apple announced Monday that it plans to invest more than $500 billion in the United States over the next four years, including plans to hire 20,000 people and build a new server factory in Texas.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Massive airship could shake up cargo transport
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson says this enormous airship revolutionizes cargo transport by flying to remote areas.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
